content.problemStatement:
After getting lost in Hollywood, ibti decided to give you yet another problem related to permutations.

Consider a permutation PPP of length 2‚ãÖN2\cdot N2‚ãÖN.

An array AAA of size NNN is generated using the permutation PPP, such that the ithi^{th}ith element of the array is a **pair** having value, Ai=(Pi‚Äämod‚ÄäN,P(i+N)‚Äämod‚ÄäN)A_i = (P_i \bmod N, P_{(i+N)} \bmod N)Ai‚Äã=(Pi‚ÄãmodN,P(i+N)‚ÄãmodN).

The permutation PPP (of length 2‚ãÖN2\cdot N2‚ãÖN) is called NNN-beautiful, if, all elements of the array AAA are **distinct**.

Given integers KKK and MMM, your task is to count the number of iii-beautiful permutations for each 1‚â§i‚â§K1 \le i \le K1‚â§i‚â§K.

Since the answers can be large, print them modulo MMM.


Input Format
The first and only line of the input contains two space-separated integers KKK, MMM - the parameters defined in the statement.



Output Format
Print KKK space-separated integers, the ithi^{th}ith integer will denote the number of iii-beautiful permutations modulo MMM.
constraints: It is guaranteed that MMM is a prime number.
